channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will 'go right back to dropping bombs' if he doesn't like Iran deal
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said Wednesday at the G7 conference that the U.S. will "go right back to dropping bombs" if he doesn't like the Iran deal.

item.title: Fed meeting live updates: Here's everything to expect from Kevin Warsh's first rate decision
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s rate decision on Wednesday marks the first one with Kevin Warsh at the helm.

item.title: Carvana’s new vehicle strategy turns dealership into ‘playground,’ test-drive center with sales all online
item.description: But don't expect Carvana to sell you a vehicle at one of its seven Stellantis franchised dealerships, marking a stark contrast from typical franchised dealers.

item.title: S&P 500 futures are little changed as oil ticks higher, traders await Fed rate decision: Live updates
item.description: Wednesday's FOMC decision marks the first with new Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh at the helm of the U.S. central bank.

item.title: 'Passive' investors who dodged bitcoin are now forced to own SpaceX shares three times more volatile
item.description: Many advisors and money-managers invested in index funds who will soon be owners of Elon Musk's astronomic ambitions

item.title: Micron has more than tripled in 2026. Deutsche Bank says the rally isn't over
item.description: Micron Technology is poised to see even more upside as a memory shortage continues to be a boon for its business, per Deutsche Bank.

item.title: BMW stock slumps to 5-year low as Iran war and China slowdown spark profit warning
item.description: BMW blamed a slowdown in its Chinese market, as well as disruption from the Iran war, as two reasons for the slashed guidance.

item.title: 'A signal of where power sits': Trump and world leaders joined by OpenAI, Anthropic, Google at G7
item.description: Frontier AI risks, infrastructure and sovereignty are all expected to be discussed at the world leaders' summit.

item.title: Abercrombie & Fitch begins selling third-party shoe brands in latest bid to chase growth
item.description: Abercrombie & Fitch's explosive growth has slowed, leading the apparel retailer to expand into new categories to boost sales

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: CarMax, AST SpaceMobile, Lionsgate & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves premarket.

item.title: CNBC to wade further into live sports with 11 simulcast WNBA games
item.description: The simulcasts will expose the WNBA to an affluent audience on CNBC and marks a new chapter for the business news network after it spun out from Comcast.

item.title: Mortgage rates are now falling but demand is still weaker
item.description: A mixed week for mortgage rates resulted in less demand from both current homeowners and potential homebuyers.

item.title: Intel begins production of most-advanced chip, inching closer to possible Apple deal
item.description: Intel is entering production of 18A-P, its most-advanced chip node that could be the target of a coming deal with Apple

item.title: SpaceX gains 3% after surpassing Amazon in market cap
item.description: The space and AI company's stock has surged since its blockbuster IPO on Friday.

item.title: Bill limiting investors from buying homes set to speed through Congress
item.description: Top lawmakers reached an agreement on a key housing bill that would limit investor ownership, clearing the way for passage through both chambers of Congress.

item.title: Michael Burry says he's tempted to bet against SpaceX, but passes on expensive options
item.description: Burry argued the company's market capitalization had reached levels that dwarf many established businesses and fortunes.

item.title: Rivian laying off hundreds of workers amid R2 launch
item.description: Rivian said Tuesday it was laying off hundreds of workers, or less than 2% of its workforce.

item.title: These stocks should outperform as the Iran deal is finalized, says UBS
item.description: With a deal on the horizon, UBS thinks that stocks like Southwest Airlines and Eastman Chemicals could benefit from a potential settlement.

item.title: How Roku fits into Fox's future — and what investors are missing about the deal
item.description: Fox's stock dropped following its announcement that it will acquire Roku for $22 billion. Analysts still think it's a good deal.

item.title: SpaceX's blistering start still faces key tests that will determine the stock's true value
item.description: SpaceX's stock has yet to face true price discovery.

item.title: Anthropic's Fable shutdown is a big moment for open-source AI
item.description: Anthropic’s Fable shutdown could boost open-source AI, but many of the models gaining traction are Chinese.

item.title: What are VA loan eligibility requirements and the best lenders?
item.description: VA loans require 0% down and offer lower-than-average rates. Here's what you need to know about eligibility and lenders.

item.title: Databricks sales growth tops 80%, but margin are shrinking from swarm of AI agents
item.description: Databricks is seeing higher growth as AI agents assist with data analysis, but all that activity is significantly increasing costs.

item.title: DOJ assists Musk's xAI in NAACP air pollution suit, asks court to toss case
item.description: The DOJ is asking a Mississippi federal court to dismiss a lawsuit filed by the NAACP against Elon Musk's xAI, now owned by SpaceX.

item.title: Central banks are bringing gold reserves home as geopolitical risks rise
item.description: Central banks expect gold reserves to rise and more are storing bullion at home amid geopolitical and currency risks.

item.title: Jim Cramer says SpaceX investors aren't buying earnings — they're buying Elon Musk
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said SpaceX investors are betting on Elon Musk's vision and track record, rather than the company's current earnings power.

item.title: Ineos and Daimler Truck enlist in auto defense push as Europe bolsters military spending
item.description: The partnership is one of a spate of auto-defense collaborations announced in recent weeks.

item.title: China pushes for AI safety as G7 summit wraps up without Beijing
item.description: China included artificial intelligence in its global governance whitepaper published Wednesday, and said Beijing was speeding up cooperation efforts.

item.title: UK inflation holds steady at 2.8% in May
item.description: The Bank of England is due to update its monetary policy on Thursday.

item.title: Japan May exports grow at fastest pace in over three years, beating estimates, as chip demand soars
item.description: Japan's exports in May grew at their fastest pace since November 2022, boosted by robust demand for cars and semiconductors.
